mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor demand that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples over the following quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or the threshold crossing fails to predict recession indicators within 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both decline > 1.0% month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint decline signal fails to produce negative forward returns in at least 55% of out-of-sample instances over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, rising consumer credit balances indicate resilient household spending that offsets tighter financial conditions, supporting positive equity returns over a 5-day horizon as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS growth when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5% (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Continuous growth in real GDP indicates expanding aggregate demand, which directly supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings estimates are revised upward.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 40% of observations, or the signal's predictive power decays to zero after controlling for earnings revisions.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 250, it signals entrenched inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and driving negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral in more than 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL crosses the 250 threshold, indicating the signal does not capture market pricing of inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising employment levels combined with stagnant industrial production indicate a structural shift toward services, which reduces capital expenditure cycles and dampens cyclical equity returns over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises while INDPRO remains flat (< 0.2% change)
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative forward returns in at least 55% of out-of-sample instances over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain normalization phases, rising industrial production paired with stable unemployment indicates improved capacity utilization without wage-push inflation, supporting positive equity returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive correlation with INDPRO growth when UNRATE is stable, or the signal produces negative returns in more than 60% of triggered instances.
